design_philosophy.core_loop: Plan mission parameters, execute combat flight operations within dynamic campaign, manage limited squadron resources across fictional war scenarios
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing authentic flight dynamics mastery against resource scarcity (pilots, aircraft, weapons) in persistent campaign engine
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command authentic F-16 Fighting Falcon operations with military-grade fidelity; transition from Red Flag training to real combat scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising commitment to flight model authenticity over accessibility—requires math coprocessor, 386+ minimum, refuses to simplify aerodynamics for casual play. Designers deliberately targeted simulation enthusiasts willing to invest in mastery rather than arcade audiences

difficulty.lives_system: Squadron-based: limited supply of pilots and aircraft replenished periodically; pilot loss permanent until resupply
difficulty.continues_system: Campaign persistence—failed missions affect resource pool; no traditional continue/restart at mission level
difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign-level checkpointing with dynamic resource management; individual missions lack mid-mission saves
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extensive difficulty configuration through flight modes (high-fidelity with math coprocessor vs. arcade approximations) and campaign scenario selection; Red Flag tutorial provides structured skill progression

progression.upgrade_system: Aircraft variant unlock through campaign progression (standard F-16A baseline, FSX Japanese variant in expansion); weapons loadout customization per mission
progression.unlock_mechanism: Expansion scenarios unlock new theaters (Iraq, Israel, Panama, future Japan-Russia conflict); Art of the Kill video tutorial unlocks dogfighting fundamentals
replayability_hooks: Fictional war campaign engine with dynamic resource depletion creates unique run conditions
Multiple campaign theaters (Iraq, Israel, Panama) with distinct geopolitical scenarios
Red Flag training exercise as structured tutorial repeatable for skill refinement
Multiplayer via null modem serial connection for competitive 1v1 dogfighting
ACMI recorder for analyzing and reconstructing engagements post-mission
Expansion scenarios introducing new aircraft and future conflict hypotheticals

world_building_techniques: Geopolitical scenario framing (Iraq, Israel, Panama conflicts; Japan-Russia future war)
Campaign engine context: limited supplies create tension of sustaining multi-mission operations
Real military training exercise integration (Red Flag Nevada) grounds fiction in authentic doctrine
Topographic authenticity (unique mountain, hill, valley formations) reinforces geographic realism

faction_indicators: Theater selection (Iraq, Israel, Panama, Japan-Russia) implicitly aligns player with specific geopolitical actors
F-16 vs. MiG-29 matchup in expansion materials signals Cold War tension narratives
danger_signals: Campaign resource depletion mechanic creates existential stakes for squadron survival
Permanent pilot loss reinforces consequence weight—failure cascades into future operations
Limited aircraft/weapons replenishment forces strategic triage decisions
environmental_storytelling: Terrain authenticity (Iraq, Israel, Panama geography) establishes mission context without exposition
Topographic landmarks create natural navigation challenges and environmental hazards
Cockpit avionics fidelity communicates military institutional complexity implicitly
